cards.front: isAlive() method checks the state of a Thread object, indicating whether the thread is alive or not
cards.back: True if the thread is alive; False otherwise.

cards.front: The join() method blocks the calling thread until the thread that is joined is no longer alive
cards.back: Blocks the calling thread until the thread that is joined is no longer alive.

cards.front: Thread priorities determine the order in which threads are executed by the JVM
cards.back: Lower priority threads are executed first; Higher priority threads are executed next.

cards.front: Synchronization is used to coordinate the access of shared resources among multiple threads
cards.back: Ensures that only one thread can access shared resources at a time.

cards.front: Thread communication methods include using synchronization, using wait() and notify() methods, and using locks
cards.back: Use synchronization, wait() and notify() methods, and locks to communicate between threads.
